title: pavonia fonsecana
intro: Pavonia fonsecana, also known as the Mexican rose mallow, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the hibiscus family (Malvaceae). The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Pavonia fonsecana include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever and inflammation, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by mixing its extracts with a solvent, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine.
